0001.title: Run Two Review Instances Through a v2 Workflow

result.summary: Implemented the portable workflow v2 vertical slice. Fresh Issues now run Development → Security Review → Final Review → QA through UUID-keyed catalog dispatch, typed bindings, independent review sessions and attempts, generic persisted runtime state, canonical workflow snapshots, and distinct dashboard rows.
residual_risks: The configured pytest and Ruff tools were unavailable in the sandbox; all 205 discoverable standard-library tests and focused workflow tests passed.

result.summary: The happy path and all 205 tests pass, but failure handling can bypass the configured two-review workflow, loses review evidence, and does not reliably enforce the persisted workflow snapshot.
findings: HIGH — Workflow gate bypass: src/devloop/cli.py:398 sends only fresh attempts through the v2 executor, while clean retries at src/devloop/cli.py:779 supply initial_fix_list and attempt_label and therefore fall into the legacy coder → one reviewer → QA path. A focused diagnostic reproduced a Security Review failure followed by successful legacy retry completion without Security Review and Final Review both passing.

HIGH — Review evidence is discarded: src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:394 emits outputs only for SUCCEEDED, while CHANGES_REQUESTED records receive no blocked_reason, failure_reason, or rework reference. Persistence at src/devloop/state.py:141 consequently stores an empty record without the review findings or fix list. The reproduced clean retry reported that no actionable blocker was recorded.

HIGH — Snapshot integrity is not enforced on the real execution path: src/devloop/state.py:64 overwrites an existing resolved_workflow when its stored hash equals the supplied default without first validating stored content. A diagnostic showed a tampered snapshot being silently replaced. Additionally, src/devloop/cli.py:616 executes a newly constructed default rather than the validated persisted snapshot.

MEDIUM — Portable status consumers still encode legacy strings: src/devloop/state.py:114 now persists enum values such as COMPLETED and IN_PROGRESS, but src/devloop/interactive_runner.py:806 and src/devloop/interactive_runner.py:921 recognize only Completed, Blocked, and In Progress. Portable /status and resume-candidate activity can therefore misreport v2 runs.
fix_list: Keep clean retries inside the configured v2 workflow and add failure-path tests proving an issue cannot complete unless both configured review instances execute successfully.
Persist the complete structured result or typed rework artifact for CHANGES_REQUESTED and blocker details for BLOCKED; verify findings and fix lists survive state round trips.
Validate any existing workflow document against its stored hash before writing, and execute the validated resolved workflow from loop state rather than reconstructing the default.
Update portable state readers to parse IssueStatus values while retaining legacy-state compatibility where required, with status and resume-candidate regression tests.
residual_risks: The authenticated real-Codex integration gate was not run, consistent with repository managed-shell policy.

verification_commands: Focused inline reproductions for blocked-rework retry and interrupted-attempt recovery
findings: HIGH: The executable wrapper path discards the exact triggering Step Attempt Record. `src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:162` supplies `rework_attempt_record`, but `src/devloop/cli.py:795` removes it before `CodexRunner.run_role`; `src/devloop/codex_runner.py:351-375` and its prompt builder have no equivalent input. The real coder receives only a flattened fix list, contrary to the exact-record acceptance criterion.

HIGH: A blocked rework Development attempt loses its triggering review record. `src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:506-507` clears pending rework after every coder result, including BLOCKED/FAILED, while `src/devloop/state.py:230-242` restores pending rework only when the latest outcome is CHANGES_REQUESTED. A focused persisted-retry reproduction confirmed the next Development attempt received neither `fix_list` nor `rework_attempt_record`.

HIGH: Interrupted executions are not retained in attempt history. The attempt is created only after role execution returns at `src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:491-549`; recovery generates a new prompt session and overwrites the running runtime state at `src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:459-466`. A focused reproduction confirmed the interrupted session disappeared after resume, violating the inspectable-history requirement.
fix_list: Carry an allowlisted serialization of the exact triggering Step Attempt Record through `CodexRunner.run_role`, prompt construction, and the coder prompt; add wrapper-level tests for both review instances.
Retain pending rework until Development succeeds, and restore it across blocked, failed, and cancelled retries using the persisted trigger/link. Add blocked-rework round-trip tests.
Persist active attempt identity before execution and preserve interrupted-attempt evidence when resuming or starting a recovery attempt. Assert the original session/attempt remains inspectable.

findings: HIGH — `src/devloop/state.py:847`: `load_existing_state` silently converts unreadable, malformed, or non-object existing state into a fresh run. The next workflow resolution can overwrite that state with a new snapshot, losing attempt history instead of failing clearly before execution. An inline probe confirmed malformed JSON was reset and replaced.

MEDIUM — `src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:777`: workflow validation does not reject unknown top-level fields. Because hashing occurs after loading and normalizing the document, extra persisted fields are discarded before hash comparison and therefore evade tamper detection. An inline probe confirmed an unexpected root field was accepted with the existing canonical hash semantics.
fix_list: Treat only a nonexistent state file as a new run. Raise an actionable error for unreadable, malformed, or non-object existing state and add regression tests proving the file remains untouched.
Validate the exact supported v2 top-level field set before normalization. Add a state round-trip tampering test showing unknown root fields are rejected rather than hidden by canonicalization.

findings: HIGH — Custom installed components are not available through the production editor or executor. src/devloop/interactive_runner.py:423 invokes the editor without a discovered catalog, src/devloop/workflow_editor.py:276 falls back to the three hard-coded components from src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:207, and src/devloop/cli.py:354 and :667 use that same catalog during execution. tests/test_workflow_editor.py:88 only passes a synthetic catalog directly, so it does not cover the actual /options path. A custom workflow component therefore cannot be selected and executed as required.

HIGH — Reordering can persist a workflow whose required producer executes after its consumer. WorkflowDraft.move at src/devloop/workflow_editor.py:147 rewires only SUCCEEDED transitions, while retained bindings are neither reevaluated nor reported by _unresolved_input_lines at :655. Moving QA to position 2 applied successfully, but PortableWorkflowExecutor then raised RuntimeError because Final Review had not produced review_result. Existing move tests only swap reviewers with equivalent upstream dependencies.
fix_list: Build the portable component catalog from installed roles, skills, and step adapters, and pass the same catalog through /options, persistence, snapshot loading, dry runs, and execution. Add a production-path integration test for a discoverable custom component.

After a Primary Path move, detect bindings whose producers are no longer executable upstream. Leave them explicitly unresolved or block Apply without guessing, and add regression tests that move QA before Final Review and Development after its consumers.

findings: MEDIUM / Security / MUST_FIX — Step display names accept multiline and terminal-control content at `src/devloop/portable_component_catalog.py:120` and `src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:825`, then render it unsanitized at `src/devloop/workflow_editor.py:600` and `src/devloop/statusui.py:155`. Loading `\x1b[2JInjected\nHeading` reproduced the escape sequence unchanged in editor output. Custom metadata or persisted configuration can manipulate the terminal and corrupt generated Markdown headings.

Inline explicitly permitted FAILED-binding editor reproduction
findings: HIGH — src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:677-693: when an edge closes a cycle at the pass limit, the executor discards the configured destination and derives terminal status from the last outcome. A branch whose SUCCEEDED edge reconnects to an earlier step is therefore reported as COMPLETED even though that transition is not terminal. The reproduction executed Development -> Security Review (BLOCKED) -> Recovery Development (SUCCEEDED), then returned COMPLETED while Recovery Development still targeted Security Review. src/devloop/cli.py:704-706 consequently marks the issue and acceptance criteria complete without reaching the successful terminal path.

MEDIUM — src/devloop/workflow_editor.py:1149-1177 validates the displayed current binding against compatible_port_bindings, but src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:1195-1205 only constructs default SUCCEEDED bindings. A loader-valid binding with allowed_outcomes: [FAILED] is displayed as incompatible or unavailable, while its permitted outcome remains hidden. The Advanced editor therefore reports a false validation error for a valid typed binding.
fix_list: Represent cycle-budget exhaustion explicitly and never map it to COMPLETED merely because the closing edge outcome was SUCCEEDED. Return a non-success status with an actionable exhaustion reason, and add direct and recovered regressions for a mixed-outcome cycle closed by SUCCEEDED.

Validate existing bindings using their own allowed_outcomes and display those outcomes. Keep ordinary auto-binding defaulted to SUCCEEDED, but separate default candidate generation from validation of explicitly permitted non-success bindings; add FAILED/BLOCKED editor regressions.

findings: HIGH — Fast capability detection uses the wrong catalog identifier. src/devloop/model_catalog.py:18 and :71 recognize only service-tier ID "fast". Codex 0.144.4 bundled metadata advertises Fast as additionalSpeedTiers=["fast"] and serviceTiers=[{"id":"priority","name":"Fast"}], so supported models produce supports_fast=False and the editor/preflight reject Fast On. tests/test_model_catalog.py:65 uses a non-current synthetic ID and masks the defect.

HIGH — Catalog preflight runs in a different configuration context from execution. src/devloop/model_catalog.py:385 starts app-server without the target repository as cwd, while src/devloop/codex_runner.py:111 executes with -C repo_root. Project .codex/config.toml, model_catalog_json, provider, or managed constraints can therefore differ between authorization and execution.

HIGH — Analysis is not independently configurable or persisted. src/devloop/chat_loop.py:86 hard-codes its settings, src/devloop/interactive_runner.py:150 never loads them from the User Workflow Default, and src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:1420 defines a workflow beginning at Development. Analysis is consequently absent from editor persistence, immutable snapshots, and preflight, contrary to the issue's every-Codex-backed-step requirement.

MEDIUM — Required catalog failure coverage is incomplete. tests/test_model_catalog.py covers pagination and cache only; it lacks current Fast metadata, hidden models, empty results, malformed responses, repeated cursors, and adapter connection failures requested by the PRD testing decisions.
fix_list: Separate the catalog's advertised Fast capability from the CLI service_tier override. Support the current priority/Fast metadata and deprecated additionalSpeedTiers fallback, then add schema-aligned regression tests.
Give CodexModelCatalogAdapter the target repository/worktree cwd and use it consistently from planning, handoff, and implementation preflight.
Represent Analysis as a persisted configurable step, load its snapshotted settings into ChatConfig for fresh/resumed turns, and validate it against a fresh catalog before execution.
Add the missing malformed, empty, hidden, pagination-loop, connection-failure, and current Fast-metadata tests.
residual_risks: No authenticated live catalog smoke test was launched, per repository policy; protocol verification used Codex 0.144.4's bundled catalog, generated schema, help, and the current official manual.

result.summary: Automated gates pass, but blocking acceptance and correctness gaps remain around Execution Budget, immutable Analysis settings, catalog recovery, stale-cache enforcement, and configured backend propagation.
verification_commands: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_model_catalog tests.test_codex_execution_settings tests.test_codex_runner tests.test_chat_loop tests.test_interactive_runner tests.test_portable_workflow tests.test_workflow_editor tests.test_cli_banners tests.test_resume -q
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -q
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/devloop-review-pycache PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m compileall -q src/devloop tests
findings: HIGH — Execution Budget is not implemented or independently configurable. WorkflowStep and its persisted schema contain only codex_settings, transitions, and bindings (src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:379 and src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:1006), while the editor has no timeout/checkpoint budget controls. This leaves the explicit acceptance criterion and PRD decision unmet.

HIGH — Analysis executes from one in-memory workflow snapshot, but that snapshot is discarded before development. src/devloop/interactive_runner.py:159 loads workflow_snapshot and line 198 uses its Analysis settings; after planning, line 231 calls run_handoff without that snapshot, and src/devloop/cli.py:178 reloads the possibly changed User Workflow Default into run state. Applying /options during Analysis can therefore make persisted resolved_workflow claim settings different from those actually used for Analysis.

HIGH — Preflight failures do not provide the promised route to /options or Retry Catalog. src/devloop/interactive_runner.py:157-178 exits through parser.error before the chat and its /options callback are created at line 200. src/devloop/cli.py:164-186 similarly exits while instructing users to open an unavailable command. An unavailable built-in/default model can therefore prevent users from reaching the editor needed to repair it.

MEDIUM — Cached catalog data is not display-only. After live discovery fails, src/devloop/workflow_editor.py:929 loads the stale cache, while _selected_codex_context at lines 865-884 accepts it without requiring is_fresh; model/reasoning/Fast handlers then mutate and Apply the workflow. A temporary-directory probe confirmed a cached-only model was persisted after live discovery failed. Cache-backed mutation must be disabled even though later run preflight is fresh.

MEDIUM — The configured Codex executable is not propagated across the planning handoff. Initial discovery and Analysis use args.codex at src/devloop/interactive_runner.py:164 and line 193, but handoff catalog discovery hardcodes "codex" at line 742 and build_devloop_args at lines 634-664 omits --codex. With a custom backend path, catalog authorization and subsequent execution can use different installations.
fix_list: Add a per-step Execution Budget contract with component defaults, workflow/default/run-state persistence, editor controls, runtime enforcement, and round-trip tests proving it remains independent of model settings.

Carry the exact preflighted workflow snapshot from Analysis through handoff into the immutable loop state; add a test that edits Future Runs during Analysis and proves the current run retains the original settings.
Provide an accessible preflight-repair loop that can open /options, retry live discovery, and revalidate before execution; do not emit an unreachable recovery instruction.
Require a fresh catalog for model, reasoning, and Fast mutations. Keep cached entries visible only for stale display and add a regression proving cached-only choices cannot be persisted.
Propagate the selected --codex executable through handoff catalog discovery and the generated devloop arguments, with a custom-executable regression test.
residual_risks: No authenticated live catalog or Codex execution smoke test was launched, per repository safety policy; review used the installed Codex 0.144.4 CLI help/features and local generated App Server schema.
Requested CONTEXT-MAP.md, docs/TDD/README.md, and context/* documents are absent from this checkout; the self-improvement wiki records this as a known prompt-preflight gap.

result.summary: Automated gates pass, but Execution Budget enforcement is not authoritative for a complete step attempt and does not safely terminate subprocess trees.
verification_commands: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_model_catalog tests.test_codex_execution_settings tests.test_codex_runner tests.test_chat_loop tests.test_interactive_runner tests.test_portable_workflow tests.test_workflow_editor tests.test_cli_banners tests.test_resume -q
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -b -q
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/devloop-review-pycache PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m compileall -q src/devloop tests
findings: HIGH — Execution Budget restarts for every internal connection retry instead of governing the complete Step Attempt. `src/devloop/codex_runner.py:540-573` creates a new process budget per retry and excludes retry backoff from both deadlines. An adversarial probe using a 0.2-second timeout and two 0.15-second retry delays returned success after 0.350 seconds and three executions. A retryable outage can therefore exceed the configured timeout indefinitely.

HIGH — Budget expiration and catalog cleanup terminate only the immediate launcher process. `src/devloop/subprocess_utils.py:104-118` does not terminate descendants, while `src/devloop/codex_runner.py:240-244` waits for stdout EOF; `src/devloop/model_catalog.py:443-484` has equivalent parent-only App Server cleanup. A 0.2-second budget probe with a child retaining inherited pipes returned only after 2.028 seconds. Custom wrappers and Windows npm launchers can consequently outlive timeouts or become orphaned.
fix_list: Use one attempt-level deadline and activity checkpoint across all internal retries, including backoff, and stop retrying when it expires. Add a regression proving retryable failures cannot return PASS after the attempt budget.
Own and terminate the complete Codex process tree for streaming execution and model-catalog discovery on POSIX and Windows. Add child-retains-pipe and catalog-timeout cleanup tests.
residual_risks: No authenticated live catalog or real Codex execution smoke test was run, per repository safety policy; Codex 0.144.4 help and generated protocol schemas were checked locally.
CONTEXT-MAP.md, docs/TDD/README.md, and the requested context/ files are absent from this checkout; available root context and ADRs were reviewed.

Inline portable-log recovery reproduction through LoopStateWriter.resume_issue (observed cursor: qa, pass 1)
findings: HIGH / Correctness / MUST_FIX: `src/devloop/state.py:52-53` now treats any prefixed coder/reviewer/qa result as a legacy role log. This matches portable per-step filenames generated at `src/devloop/codex_runner.py:480-497`. Because legacy recovery runs before portable checkpoint recovery at `src/devloop/cli.py:706-725`, resuming an interrupted custom workflow can incorrectly enter the hard-coded legacy cursor. A reproduction with portable Development and Security Review logs resumed directly at legacy QA instead of by Step Instance ID.
fix_list: Keep portable per-step attempt artifacts out of `recover_role_passes`, preserving a distinct and unambiguous legacy log contract.
Add a crash/resume regression covering portable logs and repeated/custom review steps, asserting recovery uses the persisted Step Instance ID and generic workflow checkpoint.
residual_risks: Windows subprocess-tree cleanup was inspection-only in this Linux environment; descendant cleanup after the root process has already exited remains unverified on Windows.

findings: MEDIUM/MUST_FIX — src/devloop/codex_runner.py:902 truncates a slug after stripping delimiters. A valid display name such as 47 `a` characters followed by ` b` therefore emits a 48-character token ending in `-`, producing `--` before the Step Instance ID. PORTABLE_ROLE_LOG_PATTERN at src/devloop/state.py:61 rejects that generated filename, while NORMAL_ROLE_LOG_PATTERN at src/devloop/state.py:55 classifies it as a legacy reviewer artifact. Add a regression using actual CodexRunner-generated artifacts with display names and custom roles whose truncation boundary lands on a separator; verify recover_role_passes ignores those portable artifacts.

findings: HIGH — src/devloop/portable_workflow.py:1585, src/devloop/workflow_editor.py:287, src/devloop/workflow_editor.py:394, and src/devloop/workflow_editor.py:430: scope validation only rejects ISSUE-to-WORKFLOW transitions, so Apply accepts duplicating Analysis, deleting Analysis, and changing Analysis to an issue-scoped Type. Prefer removing the fixed ANALYSIS_STEP_ID assumption and executing every configured WORKFLOW-scoped step exactly once; otherwise block unsupported duplicate, delete, and Type Change operations before Apply. verification_commands: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow_progress tests.test_statusui tests.test_cli_banners tests.test_chat_loop tests.test_interactive_runner

PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m py_compile src/devloop/statusui.py src/devloop/cli.py src/devloop/chat_loop.py src/devloop/interactive_runner.py tests/test_workflow_progress.py tests/test_statusui.py tests/test_cli_banners.py tests/test_chat_loop.py tests/test_interactive_runner.py

PYTHONPATH=src python3 -c "from devloop.chat_loop import _render_codex_stream_line; from devloop.statusui import _safe_progress_text; assert '\x1b[2J' in _render_codex_stream_line('{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"type\":\"agent_message\",\"text\":\"\\u001b[2Jowned\"}}'); assert '\u009b' in _safe_progress_text('\u009b2Jowned'); assert '\u202e' in _safe_progress_text('safe \u202eowned')"
findings: HIGH/MUST_FIX — Terminal escape injection remains in planning and implementation output. `src/devloop/chat_loop.py:256-266` writes rendered agent text directly to stdout, while `src/devloop/cli.py:1147-1160` interpolates model-controlled `RoleResult.summary` directly into stdout/stderr. Reviewer probes confirmed that `\u001b[2Jowned` reaches both sinks unchanged. A prompt-influenced response can therefore clear or manipulate a terminal, and redirected output violates the explicit no-cursor-control requirement.

MEDIUM/MUST_FIX — The new safe-activity boundary is incomplete. `src/devloop/statusui.py:256-263` removes ESC-prefixed CSI and C0/DEL only, preserving C1 controls such as U+009B CSI and Unicode format controls such as U+202E bidi override. The planning path feeds agent text into this sanitizer at `src/devloop/chat_loop.py:257-260`, and it is rendered at `src/devloop/statusui.py:520-524`. The probe produced `AI › ok\x9b2Jspoof\u202eabc`, allowing screen manipulation on C1-aware consumers and deceptive log presentation.
fix_list: Create a shared terminal-text sanitation boundary and apply it before every console write of agent/backend/result text. Remove or visibly escape ESC/C1 control families and Unicode bidi/format controls while preserving intended ordinary Unicode and, where appropriate, newlines.

Add TTY and redirected-output regressions covering agent messages, dashboard activity, and role summaries containing ESC CSI, OSC, C1 CSI/OSC/ST, and bidi overrides. Assert that no executable control characters remain and normal Unicode text is preserved.
